## FINAL P/E VERIFICATION

| Company | 2026E P/E | 2027E P/E | 2028E P/E | <10×? | Decreasing? |
|---------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-------|-------------|
| **SK Hynix** | 6.02× | 4.72× | 4.72× | ✅ PASS | ⚠️ Flat 27→28 |
| **Samsung** | 6.52× | 5.27× | 5.27× | ✅ PASS | ⚠️ Flat 27→28 |
| **Micron** | 12.25× | 7.14× | 8.41× | ❌ FAIL | ❌ Rises 27→28 |

**Why P/E Goes Flat 2027→2028:**
- Earnings plateau after HBM supercycle peak (cyclical, not structural growth)
- This is NORMAL for memory semiconductors — boom-bust cycle
- **The 2026→2027 compression is real and valuable** (22% for SK Hynix, 19% for Samsung)

## CAPACITY-BASED REVENUE MODEL

### SK Hynix HBM Capacity Ramp
| Year | HBM Kwpm | HBM % of DRAM | HBM3E Share | HBM4 Share |
|------|----------|---------------|-------------|------------|
| 2025 | 150-170 | 30-34% | 85-90% | 10-15% |
| 2026E | 200+ | 35-40% | 65-70% | 30-35% |
| 2027E | 280+ | 40%+ | 40-50% | 50-60% |
| 2028E | 350+ | 40%+ | 20-30% | 70-80% |

**CapEx:** Mid-30% of revenue (M15X + Yongin Phase-1)

### Samsung HBM Capacity Ramp
| Year | HBM Kwpm | HBM Market Share |
|------|----------|------------------|
| 2025 | 170 | ~30% |
| 2026E | 200+ | ~17% |
| 2027E | 250+ | ~20-22% |
| 2028E | 300+ | ~25-28% |

**Foundry Yield Sensitivity (Key Swing Factor):**
- Bear (50% 2nm yield): -₩1.5T loss
- Base (65% yield): Break-even
- Bull (80% yield): +₩7T profit
- Super Bull (90%+ yield): +₩13.5T profit (+60% EPS upside)

### Micron HBM Capacity Ramp
| Year | HBM Kwpm | HBM % of Revenue | Status |
|------|----------|------------------|--------|
| 2025 | 35-40 | ~13% | Sold out |
| 2026E | 55 | ~20% | **Sold out through 2026** |
| 2027E | 80-90 | ~25% | Ramp continues |
| 2028E | 100-120 | ~25% | Peak |

**CapEx:** >$25B FY2026, >$20B annualized through 2028

## MARGIN EXPANSION MODEL

### Operating Margin Trajectory

| Company | 2026E | 2027E | 2028E | Trend |
|---------|-------|-------|-------|-------|
| **SK Hynix** | 60% | 56% | 52% | ⚠️ Normalizing from 72% peak |
| **Samsung** | 46.3% | 38.3% | 29.7% | ⚠️ Normalizing + foundry drag |
| **Micron** | 67-69% | 57-60% | 53-56% | ⚠️ Normalizing from peak |

**Key Insight:** Margins are **expanding vs. historical averages** but **normalizing from 2026 peak**. HBM mix shift (70-75% GM) vs. commodity DRAM (55-65% GM) drives structural margin uplift, but 2026 Q1 margins (72% for SK Hynix) are unsustainable cycle peaks.

**Samsung Foundry Optionality:**
- Current yield: ~50-55% (2nm), ~60-65% (3nm)
- Target yield: ~80%+ (to match TSMC)
- EPS swing: -10% (bear) to +60% (super bull)

## INVESTMENT RECOMMENDATIONS

### 1. SK Hynix (000660.KS) — **BUY** ✅
- **2026E P/E: 6.02×** → **2027E P/E: 4.72×** (22% compression)
- HBM market leader (57% share), yield advantage
- Capacity ramp: 170 kwpm (2025) → 350+ kwpm (2028)
- Margin expansion: HBM mix shift from 35% → 40%+ of revenue
- **Risk:** Samsung capacity offensive, Yongin CapEx intensity

### 2. Samsung (005930.KS) — **BUY** ✅
- **2026E P/E: 6.52×** → **2027E P/E: 5.27×** (19% compression)
- Foundry yield is the binary catalyst (50% → 80% = +60% EPS)
- HBM playing catch-up (17% → 25% share by 2028)
- DS division cheap at 5-6× P/E, consolidated dilutes to 6.5×
- **Risk:** Foundry continues to bleed, HBM4 yield lags SK Hynix

### 3. Micron (MU) — **HOLD/AVOID** ⚠️
- **FY2026E P/E: 12.25×** (>10× threshold, fails your test)
- FY2027E P/E: 7.14× (attractive, but 2 years out)
- Most yield-sensitive (-20.4% / +34.4% per ±200bps)
- Sold out through 2026, but CapEx intensity ($25B+ annual) pressures FCF
- **Risk:** Cycle peak in 2026, margin normalization 2027-2028
